      • widgetTypeName(w: NavigatorWidget): string

Navigator: a stack of routes. Each route is a widget builder. The Navigator paints only the topmost route. push adds a route; pop removes the top.

Routes are widgets, not heavyweight Route objects (Flutter has both). For the 90% case of "show this screen next, return when done", a widget builder is enough.

A global navigator key gives any widget access to push / pop without prop-drilling, identical in shape to Flutter's Navigator.of(context) shortcut.

Public surface:

  • navigator(initial): build a Navigator with an initial route.
  • Navigator.push(builder): push a new route.
  • Navigator.pop(result): pop the top route, optionally returning a result to whoever pushed it.
  • currentNavigator(): app-wide handle to the active navigator.

Imports

../foundation/widget, ../foundation/render_object, ../foundation/key, ../foundation/runtime, transitions

Types

NavigatorHandle = ref object
  state*: NavigatorState
Lightweight handle a widget can hold to drive navigation without referencing the State directly. Becomes invalid when the navigator is disposed. 
NavigatorState = ref object of State
  stack*: seq[RouteBuilder]
  results*: seq[proc (value: pointer) {.closure.}]
NavigatorWidget = ref object of StatefulWidget
  initialRoute*: RouteBuilder
RouteBuilder = proc (): Widget {.closure.}

Vars

activeNavigator: NavigatorHandle
App-wide handle to the most-recently-mounted Navigator. Read via currentNavigator(). Mounting a second Navigator replaces the handle; the old one keeps its state but isn't reachable via the global.

Procs

proc currentNavigator(): NavigatorHandle {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
App-wide active navigator. Use to push or pop from anywhere without holding a reference. Returns nil if no Navigator is mounted. 
proc depth(h: NavigatorHandle): int {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Current stack depth. 1 means only the initial route is shown. 
proc navigator(initial: RouteBuilder; key: Key = nil): NavigatorWidget {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}

Builds a Navigator with initial as the bottom of the stack. Place near the top of your widget tree.

Inputs:

  • initial: builder for the initial / home route.
  • key: optional reconciliation key.

Effect: the topmost route in the stack is rendered. Push and pop via currentNavigator().push(...) / .pop(...).

proc pop(h: NavigatorHandle; value: pointer = nil) {....raises: [Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Pops the top route. If the route that pushed it provided an onResult, fires it with value. 
proc popUntil(h: NavigatorHandle; depth: int) {....raises: [Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Pops until the stack has exactly depth routes. Does nothing if depth >= current depth. Useful for "back to home". 
proc push(h: NavigatorHandle; route: RouteBuilder;
          transition: RouteTransitionKind = trSlideLeft;
          onResult: proc (value: pointer) = nil) {....raises: [Exception],
    tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}

Pushes route onto the stack and rebuilds. onResult fires when this route is popped (the value passed to pop).

transition wraps the new route in an animated transition widget. trSlideLeft (the default) matches iOS-style navigation. trNone skips the animation entirely. The transition runs once on mount; subsequent rebuilds of the same route do not re-animate.

proc pushReplacement(h: NavigatorHandle; route: RouteBuilder) {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Replaces the top route. The replaced route's onResult is NOT called (it didn't pop, it was replaced).

Methods

method build(s: NavigatorState; ctx: BuildContext): Widget {.
    ...raises: [Exception], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Builds and returns the topmost route. Lower routes are not built or laid out until they become topmost (push pop pattern). 
method createElement(w: NavigatorWidget): Element {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}

    forbids: [].}
